commands.review_summary:
Updates the dev test toolchain from Vitest 3.2.6 to 4.1.8, refreshes the npm lockfile to esbuild 0.28.1/Vite 8.0.16, and adjusts the OpenAI unit-test mock for Vitest 4 constructor behavior.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a dependency/security PR rather than a user bug report. The PR body reports reproducing the replaced Dependabot branch failure on Node 22.22.2, and GitHub checks for this head passed on Node 20.19.0 and Node 22.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed; the PR has no actionable review finding and only needs normal maintainer merge handling.

target.title: chore(deps): update esbuild via Vitest 4
target.body:
## Summary

- replace #8 with a maintainer-writable branch while preserving Dependabot's exact manifest and lockfile update
- update Vitest 3.2.6 to 4.1.8, which resolves Vite 8.0.16 and esbuild 0.28.1
- make the OpenAI mock constructor-compatible with Vitest 4

## Dependency audit

The repository uses npm with a v3 `package-lock.json`; there is no Yarn lockfile or package-manager override. The large lockfile diff is a real dependency-graph change, not a package-manager migration.

A lock-only update fixes the esbuild copy used by `tsx`, but Vite 7.3.5 requires esbuild `^0.27.0`, leaving the vulnerable nested copy and five high-severity `npm audit` findings. Vitest 4 permits Vite 8, which removes that nested copy and resolves esbuild 0.28.1 throughout the graph.

The advisories are development-only here: the high-severity Deno download path is not used by this Node package, and the low-severity Windows `esbuild.serve` path is not called. The update is still appropriate during stabilization because it clears the audit without changing runtime package dependencies or the packed consumer surface.

Security references:

- https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/security/advisories/GHSA-gv7w-rqvm-qjhr
- https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/security/advisories/GHSA-g7r4-m6w7-qqqr
- https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/releases/tag/v0.28.1

## Replacement provenance

Dependabot PR #8 is not maintainer-writable and fails on Node 22 because Vitest 4 requires constructor-compatible class mocks. This replacement keeps Dependabot's `package.json` and `package-lock.json` byte-for-byte and preserves `dependabot[bot]` as commit co-author.

Replaces: https://github.com/steipete/mcp-agentify/pull/8

## Verification

- reproduced #8 on Node 22.22.2: 5/5 `llmOrchestrator` tests failed with `OpenAI ... is not a constructor`
- Node 20.19.0: `npm ci`, format, lint, 34/34 tests, package check, dry-run pack, audit
- Node 22.22.2: `npm ci`, format, lint, 34/34 tests, package check, dry-run pack, audit
- `npm audit --audit-level=high`: 0 vulnerabilities
- installed the generated tarball into a temporary consumer and verified MCP stdio initialization, `orchestrate_task` discovery, and a routed backend call
- structured autoreview: clean, no actionable findings
target.author: steipete
